title: The Lane Split, Reviewed
subtitle: The desktop Live/dev lane mission delivered its core promise — both lanes proven live in the window, boundaries held, and a cold cross-provider audit that prevented destructive runtime actions. The miss sat in the acceptance layer: a dev-supervisor teardown failure observed by Worker, Manager, and Chief was still converted into a passing result and “small follow-up.”
subMeta: evidence: mission packet · EXP · four agent transcripts · message journal · PR #41 · written in house format by a second team — conversion record in the appendix

subject.verdict: PARTLY HELD — repeat once before scaling

footer: Review boundary. This was a read-only process review; the disruptive Live-window exercise was not repeated.
Format note. Transcribed into house format by a second team from the original prose review .novakai/work/mission_desktop-live-lanes/review.md — full conversion record in the appendix.

label: Team

cells: Coach; Owner; supplied direction and merge decision
Chief; compiled, staffed, ruled, accepted, reported

html: The intended line Chris ↔ Chief ↔ Manager ↔ Worker held. A Worker-to-Manager message surfaced in the Chief terminal because of a mailbox delivery defect; the journal proves the Worker did not bypass the Manager. The Manager checked the envelopes, corrected the record, and kept management in lane.

label: The Short Version

html: Verdict: PARTLY HELD — repeat once before scaling.

Chris should feel encouraged, not complacent. The new method produced a strong mission contract, clean delegation, and an excellent cross-provider audit that prevented destructive runtime actions. The core Live/dev lane split was proven in the window. The process is not ready to scale yet because Worker, Manager, and Chief each observed the same dev-supervisor teardown failure, then the acceptance chain still converted it into a passing result and “small follow-up.”

label: Evidence At A Glance

teardown evidence · Worker, Manager, Chief + unresolved PR review

label: Timeline · AEST · 21 Jul 2026
note: Single-time events render as narrow markers; no latency totals are inferred.

label: 07:53–07:58 · Chief onboarding, read-back, and Chris PASS.
08:10:58 · Chris gave the raw direction, including EXP-before-execution and the unverified 3130/3131 suggestion.
08:12–08:13 · source.md, brief.md, and the EXP were filed before any Manager spawn.
08:20–08:25 · Manager spawned as the requested provider, passed onboarding, received the Contract, returned a kickoff, and waited for GO.
08:26–08:31 · Worker spawned, passed a mission-hidden read-back, received the Contract, wrote plan.md, and stopped.
08:33–08:39 · Cold Codex audit returned exactly 3 SEVERE, 3 MODERATE, 1 LOW.

08:40–08:46 · Manager verified the runtime claims, ruled all findings, obtained the Chief's occupied-port decision, and commissioned one targeted re-check. The re-check failed and was remedied before build.
08:47 · Manager issued one build authorization with branch, ownership, hard rules, evidence, checkpoints, and stop point.
08:57 · Worker reported edits and gates complete.
09:00–09:08 · Dependency skew invalidated the old restore target; Worker held, Manager escalated, and Chief approved a fresh baseline-main restore.
09:08–09:25 · Authorized Live window, both-lane matrix, store smoke, restore, evidence filing, and PR open.
09:25–09:28 · Worker self-report, then Manager hands-on verification and result.md with recommendation ACCEPT.
09:32–09:37 · Chief inspected the diff, reran Order B and focused gates, encountered repeated concurrently respawns, and required SIGKILL to clean the dev tree. Chief accepted.
09:37:57 · PR automation independently filed an unresolved P2 on tools/dev-lane.mjs:59: the cleaner stops listeners, not the old supervisor, which can respawn them.
09:39 · Chief told Chris the result was good, the audit caught “4 severe issues,” and “Nothing broken, nothing blocked,” with teardown described as a small follow-up.
Case Study · When Manual Cleanup Became Green

html: The first meaningful weakening occurred in the acceptance layer, not in Chris's prompt or onboarding: a manually overcome teardown failure was treated as evidence of operator cleanup rather than evidence that the shipped cleanup path was incomplete.
Root cause class: Verification (primary); Execution and Management (contributing).

The Contract requires start/stop/restart isolation (brief.md:13-16), and the approved plan explicitly requires “Stop dev (SIGINT) … restart dev” (plan.md:214-223). The implementation's cleanup sends SIGTERM only to the Vite or tsx listener (tools/dev-lane.mjs:51-60), while npm run dev runs those children under concurrently --restart-tries -1 (package.json:11-12). The old supervisor can therefore recreate the listener immediately.

label: SEVERE · Verification
headline: An observed product failure was converted into a passing check
detail:
This was not a speculative edge case:

  • Worker reported two teardown leaks caused by respawning concurrently supervisors (result.md:83-84) and recorded that killing listeners is not enough in its session memory.
  • Manager reproduced the respawn race during independent teardown (msg_f2fb4e50-626c-4ecd-9db4-9ae273f47171).
  • Chief reproduced it again at 09:35–09:36; the same supervisors survived SIGTERM, spawned fresh children, and required SIGKILL.
  • The unresolved PR P2 at 09:37:57 independently described the same defect at tools/dev-lane.mjs:59.

Yet result.md marks all checks passed, and C-OWNERSHIP proves only that a foreign listener is left untouched (c-ownership-demo.txt); it never proves that a stale owned supervisor tree is reclaimed. The Chief then reported “Nothing broken, nothing blocked.” At scale, this is dangerous: a team can manually work around a defect three times and accidentally count the workaround as proof of the product behavior.

label: Requirement Chain

cells: Preserved in source.md; EXP written at 08:13, Manager spawned at 08:20
Compiled into DW-1/DW-3; preserved in Manager kickoff, plan, authorization, evidence, result, and Chief rerun
Neither lane's start/stop/restart interferes with the other
Compiled into DW-2 and an explicit start/stop/restart matrix
Partly held: Live remained safe, but dev cleanup/restart evidence contradicted the passing acceptance result
Correctly marked SHOULD in the Contract; Worker prematurely called it validated; Auditor restored runtime truth by finding 3131 occupied
Manager enforced plan-then-stop; cross-provider audit and targeted re-check both changed the plan before build
PR for Chris; agents do not merge

label: What Worked

title: Prompt compilation
body: source.md is verbatim, and brief.md correctly kept 3130/3131 as an unverified candidate while making coexistence the MUST.

title: Onboarding
body: Both read-backs produced unplanted catches. The Manager named paraphrase drift; the Worker found the two competing backend-port environment variables before seeing the mission.

title: Management boundaries
body: One Manager remained accountable; Worker status stayed with the Manager; both agents stopped at their fences.

title: Audit quality
body: The cold Auditor found the occupied 3131 owner, inherited Live-tunnel leak, and invalid snapshot control. The targeted re-check then caught an impossible /api/health restore proof. These were valuable saves, not ceremonial review.

title: Runtime decision-making
body: The team stopped for the 3131 retirement and restore-target rulings, discovered launchd as the real supervisor, and adapted the window without freelancing destructive actions.

title: Independent verification
body: Worker, Manager, and Chief each exercised real runtime behavior. The weakness was how contradictory evidence was adjudicated, not a lack of hands-on checking.

title: Culture
body: Corrections were candid and confidence-preserving. The Manager's quick envelope audit and apology to the Worker is the right pattern.

label: The Other Findings

title: MODERATE — The canonical plan contradicted itself after a material ruling
body:
Root cause class: Management.

The Chief-approved Phase 6 revision says restore fresh baseline main and prove it with an evidence bundle, not /api/health (plan.md:244-268). The same plan's completion checklist still demands the preflight-captured SHA, “NOT main,” proven via /api/health (plan.md:305-307). The build authorization (msg_6aec8f01-2596-4f25-96ac-1912a97d04a4) called “plan rev 2 + amendment” approved while also calling the plan's checklist the verbatim bar.

Execution followed the safer Chief ruling, and result.md:70-75 disclosed the deviation, so the runtime decision was sound. The process defect is artifact custody: chat plus partial edits became the real source of truth while the canonical plan retained mutually exclusive acceptance criteria.

title: MODERATE — The final report drifted from primary evidence
body:
Root cause class: Reporting; System contributed to the routing confusion.

  • audit.md:156 and journal message msg_fa345055-98fc-4a3e-9ce8-a0a6985bd2ec say exactly 3 SEVERE. The Chief report and EXP AAR say 4.
  • The message journal proves every Worker envelope went to the Manager; the Manager explicitly cleared the Worker. The Chief's later reflection still says the Worker “started DM-ing me directly.”
  • The Chief's report says “Nothing broken” after personally requiring SIGKILL for the shipped lane's repeated respawn behavior.

None of these invalidate the good lane evidence. Together they invalidate the claim that the reporting layer preserved measured truth end-to-end.

title: LOW — The packet's full-test receipt is not independently auditable
body:
Root cause class: Verification and Reporting.

evidence/gates-stage1.txt contains only four summary lines, ending ALL-TESTS-PASS; it does not record the discovered files or raw command output. The Worker transcript does show the full deterministic inventory eventually passed, so this is an evidence-quality issue, not a false test claim. Manager and Chief reran focused tests rather than the full inventory, and CI's test step covered src tests rather than the plan's src scripts tools inventory. One tee'd command receipt would have made the packet self-proving.

label: Significant Decisions And Deviations

cells: Manager verified; Chief retired the known stale owner after roster check
Inherited NVK_COMMAND_URL leaked dev agents to Live
Made the backend's actual port authoritative and added the exact regression
Captured old snapshot could not boot under current dependencies
Worker held; Manager escalated; Chief approved fresh baseline-main restore
Correct runtime decision; canonical checklist not reconciled
launchd, not the assumed process, owned Live
Window adapted through bootout/bootstrap and restored supervised Live
Owned dev supervisors respawned after listener cleanup
Worker, Manager, and Chief manually killed trees; result still accepted
Incorrectly downgraded; this is the severe process miss

label: Exact Prompt Changes Proposed

html: No onboarding change is warranted. Onboarding was one of the strongest parts of the run.

title: Chief acceptance — add the contradiction brake
body:
Before (CHIEF.md, Part 5):
“Check every named acceptance item.”

After:
“Check every named acceptance item against all observed evidence. Any failure seen by the Worker, Manager, Chief, CI, or PR review automatically reopens the related check. A manual cleanup or workaround is not a pass. It may become a follow-up only after the Manager shows that it is outside the Mission Contract. Before ACCEPT, reconcile every reported FAIL, respawn, forced kill, manual cleanup, and deviation in result.md.”

title: Build authorization — require one coherent plan
body:
Add before “Plan approved. Build is authorized”:
“Canonical-state gate: re-open plan.md after all audit and Chief rulings. The plan and its completion checklist must state one consistent decision; messages do not amend the plan. Search for and remove superseded acceptance wording before GO.”

title: Chief report — make counts and routing claims source-derived
body:
Add before the Part 6 report template:
“Re-open audit.md and result.md immediately before reporting. Copy finding counts exactly. For routing claims, use message envelopes as truth and label terminal-display anomalies as system behavior. If the summary conflicts with primary evidence, correct the summary before sending it.”

label: Smallest Useful Process Change

html: Add only the contradiction brake in Chief acceptance: one observed failure reopens its named check until fixed or explicitly proven out of scope. It needs no new artifact, meeting, role, or review stage. This single rule would have stopped the false green here while preserving the speed and warmth that worked.
For gate receipts, have the existing command tee raw output into the existing evidence directory; do not add another ceremony or document.

label: Scale Decision

html: Repeat once; do not scale the handbook yet.

Patch the contradiction brake, then run one more substantial managed mission. The next Process Reviewer should check only three things: contradictory evidence reopened a check, plan.md remained internally consistent after rulings, and the final report copied measured counts exactly. If those hold, promote and scale. The delegation skeleton has earned that second run; the acceptance gate has not yet earned autopilot.

label: How This Was Reviewed

html: This was a read-only process review. I inspected the packet, EXP, primary agent transcripts, the message journal via the backend API, the merged implementation, and PR #41. I did not repeat the disruptive Live-window exercise.
